message:
feat(models): models status reports on all embedders + storage helper

Two small changes that arrived together.

## Multi-model status reporting (src/lib.rs)

`run_models_status` previously only reported on the MiniLM-L6-v2
manifest, regardless of which embedder the resolved
`SemanticPolicy::quality_tier_embedder` would actually use. After
`cass index` was wired up to optionally use `snowflake-arctic-s` and
`nomic-embed`, `cass models status` would lie: it reported MiniLM as
the only model and either "installed" or "not installed" based purely
on MiniLM's files.

Refactored to report a per-model snapshot for every registry-known
embedder:

- `minilm` (`all-minilm-l6-v2` / 384-dim)
- `snowflake-arctic-s` (`snowflake-arctic-embed-s` / 384-dim)
- `nomic-embed` (`nomic-embed-text-v1.5` / 768-dim)

Each snapshot carries the manifest, the on-disk file inventory, the
total/installed sizes, the cache-classification report, and an
"active" boolean indicating whether the resolved policy's
`quality_tier_embedder` maps to this entry.

Policy → registry-name canonicalization handles short aliases the
policy stores ("minilm", "snowflake", "nomic", and the dimensioned
variants like "minilm-384", "snowflake-arctic-s-384",
"nomic-embed-768") and routes them to the matching registry name.
The "hash" fallback (no model files) returns `None` so the status
report still renders a clean "no semantic model selected" line.

Both `Pretty` and `Robot` (JSON / TOON) output paths consume the same
`Vec<ModelStatus>`. Pretty output groups files under their model
header and tags the active row. Robot JSON adds a top-level
`active_model` key so consumers don't have to scan the array for
`active: true`.

## storage helper: daily_stats_keys_already_ensured (src/storage/sqlite.rs)

`franken_update_ensured_daily_stats_targets_in_tx` previously checked
all four target keys against the in-memory ensured-set by acquiring
the lock four times in a row:

```rust
if !cache_keys.iter().all(|key|
    storage.daily_stats_key_already_ensured(key)
) { return Ok(false); }
```

Replaced with a single batched helper that takes the lock once and
checks all four keys under it:

```rust
fn daily_stats_keys_already_ensured(
    &self,
    keys: &[EnsuredDailyStatsKey; 4],
) -> bool {
    let ensured = self.ensured_daily_stats_keys.lock();
    keys.iter().all(|key| ensured.contains(key))
}
```

Same semantics; one mutex lock-acquire instead of four. The call site
in `franken_update_ensured_daily_stats_targets_in_tx` is updated to
the new shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
